title: Gujarat Schools and Colleges Reopen for 2026 Academic Session Amid Heatwave - Jagran Josh
full_text:
Gujarat Schools and Colleges Reopen for 2026 Academic Session Amid Heatwave
Schools and colleges across Gujarat reopened for the 2026 and 2027 academic session as students returned after summer vacation amid ongoing heatwave conditions.Read the article to know further details.
After a summer break of nearly two months schools and colleges across Gujarat reopened on Monday, officially launching the 2026-27 academic session. Government, semi-government and private institutions across the state welcomed students back, bringing life and movement back to campuses that had remained quiet during the vacation period.From the morning itself school grounds began filling up with students arriving for the first day of the new term. Classrooms, corridors and school compounds once again reflected the energy, excitement and cheerful spirit that usually mark the beginning of a fresh academic year. Read the article to get complete details.
A Cheerful Start to the New Academic Year
The reopening day created a bright and lively mood in many educational institutions. Students stepping into their new classes looked eager and happy as they reconnected with friends met their teachers and got familiar with their new surroundings.For many children the day was more than just a return to school. It was the beginning of a new class a new routine and a new set of academic goals. Across Gujarat, millions of students attended school on the very first day and started learning in their new grades.
Campuses Buzz Again with Student Activity
Educational institutions that had remained mostly silent during the holidays quickly regained their usual rhythm. Morning assemblies resumed, classroom conversations began and students once again filled school buildings with constant activity.Teachers also returned with a fresh sense of purpose. Many welcomed students warmly and expressed hope that the new academic year would be productive, smooth, and successful. Schools reported a positive environment as students gradually settled into new schedules, classrooms, and academic expectations.
Schools Reopen Even as Heatwave Continues
The new session began on schedule despite the ongoing heatwave affecting several parts of Gujarat. Since the administration did not issue any fresh order to extend the summer vacation educational institutions reopened according to the previously announced academic calendar.
School authorities and management teams moved ahead with regular academic plans while also keeping student well being in focus. The aim was to ensure that studies continue without interruption, even as weather conditions remain challenging.
Special Arrangements Introduced for Students
With high temperatures still being felt in many areas schools have taken steps to make the learning environment safer and more comfortable for students.Schools have introduced the following arrangements to make sure students stay safe and comfortable during school hours.
Student Safety Remains a Priority
Amid rising temperatures educational institutions are giving special attention to student safety and convenience. School authorities have said they will keep a close watch on the situation and continue providing facilities required to reduce heat and discomfort.
New Session Begins with Optimism Across Gujarat
As classrooms are full once again and academic activities restart across the state. The reopening has brought a renewed sense of hope, ambition and enthusiasm among students, parents and teachers. As Gujarat schools and colleges begin the new academic year, the atmosphere is one of fresh aspirations, renewed discipline and shared expectation for a successful year ahead
